section.title: Overview
section.para: The EntityLinker framework connects named entities extracted from text to external knowledge sources. For example, a person name extracted by the Name Finder can be linked to a database record, or a location entity can be resolved against a gazetteer.

Unlike other OpenNLP components that rely on pre-trained models, the EntityLinker is an extension framework. It provides the EntityLinker interface and supporting classes so that developers can implement custom linking logic for their specific use cases and data sources.

section.title: EntityLinker Tool
section.para: The command line tool runs a configured EntityLinker against sentences read from standard input. It requires a properties file that specifies which linker implementation to use for each entity type.

Usage: opennlp EntityLinker model < sentences
The model argument is the path to a properties file configuring one or more EntityLinker implementations. The tool reads tokenized and named-entity-tagged sentences from stdin and outputs the linked results.

section.title: EntityLinker API
section.para:
The core of the framework is the EntityLinker<T> interface, parameterized by a span type that extends Span. Two implementations are provided out of the box:
LinkedSpan<BaseLink> — a span with an attached list of n-best linked entries from an external source. BaseLink — an abstract class representing a minimal tuple of linked data: parent ID, item ID, item name, item type, and a score map.

section.title: Implementing a Custom EntityLinker
Configuring with Properties
section.para:
EntityLinker implementations are loaded via EntityLinkerProperties, which wraps a standard Java .properties file. The properties file specifies which implementation class to use for each entity type:
linker.location=com.example.GazetteerLinker linker.person=com.example.PersonDBLinker gazetteer.dbPath=/data/gazetteer.sqlite person.dbUrl=jdbc:mysql://localhost/persons
Use EntityLinkerFactory to instantiate a linker from a properties file:
EntityLinkerProperties props = new EntityLinkerProperties(new File("linker.properties")); EntityLinker<?> linker = EntityLinkerFactory.getLinker("location", props); List<? extends Span> linkedSpans = linker.find(docText, sentences, tokens, names);
The factory reads the linker.location key to identify the implementation class, instantiates it via the extension loader, and calls init with the properties object.

section.title: Embedding in a Pipeline
